medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient has a history of CKD, DM, HTN, with multiple episodes of cellulitis presenting with left calf swelling and pain. Recently traveled and noted gradual left lower extremity swelling for approximately 3 weeks. One day prior to presentation, developed redness and pain in the left calf along with subjective fevers. Seen by PCP and referred to ED. In ED, initial vital signs were T 99.3, P 91, BP 154/20, RR 20, O2 99%. Labs notable for WBC 18, Ht 32, Platelets 147, Cr 3. Patient treated with vancomycin, ceftriaxone, 1L NS, and 2 tabs of Percocet. Lower extremity ultrasound negative for DVT.
medical_history.past_medical_history: CKD, DM type 2, Diabetic nephropathy, Diabetic retinopathy, Hyperlipidemia, Erectile dysfunction, GERD, HTN, Morbid obesity, Gout, Cellulitis, Eczema.
medical_history.family_history: Father with heart problems, Brother with unspecified disease.

physical_examination.general_examination: Admission vital signs: T 99.5, BP 144/70, P 85, RR 20, O2 98% RA. Patient was noted as a pleasant obese male sitting up in a chair, no acute distress.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Left lower leg swollen compared to right, diffuse erythema from ankle to approximately 2cm below knee, warm to touch, no drainage or exudate. Neurological: awake and alert.
